constraints: Preserve reconstructive React/CSS output and avoid image-based shortcuts.
Keep Storybook and example-app parity through shared source paths rather than forks.
Improve dark mode and accessibility-sensitive states without flattening the visual system.
Preserve and extend reusable code/editor/diff surfaces instead of duplicating page-local code UI.
gaps: The bestiary specimen canvas, gloss annotations, and star ornament are still page-local markup/CSS in MockupPreviews instead of exported configurable Seraph primitives.
The refactor page's folio-rhythm calibration is encoded as local utility classes rather than reusable section or ledger-variant primitives.
Seraph primitive stories cover key motifs, but they still do not expose the specimen/canvas family or page-rhythm variants as first-class configurable surfaces.
DiffViewer is reusable, but its composition is still fairly monolithic for broader documentation/chat use cases and it lacks richer story controls around initial file and labels.
Dark-mode and a11y state quality exist but remain mostly CSS-convention driven; there is little component API support for alternate surface treatments and metadata hierarchy.
reusableSurfaces: SeraphWindow, SeraphSidebar, SeraphPromptBar, SeraphCard, SeraphTask, SeraphSummaryRow, SeraphComposer, SeraphAside, SeraphFolioBorder
MockupPreviews gallery surface and per-mockup story controls
specAnchors: Current shared mockup surfaces in src/mockups
Current shared code surfaces in src/components/Code

exampleSurfaces: MockupPreviews bestiary page through shared Seraph primitive usage
fixesApplied: Extracted the bestiary specimen canvas from page-local markup into a reusable exported SeraphSpecimenPlate primitive with configurable eyebrow, gloss, star ornament, and child artifact content.
Rewired the Seraph bestiary page to consume the shared specimen primitive so Storybook, examples, and page compositions can now reuse the same artifact-first surface.
Added a dedicated Seraph specimen motif story and improved DiffViewer Storybook controls so reusable manuscript and code surfaces are inspectable beyond fixed page previews.
residualRisks: The refactor-page lower ledger rhythm is still mostly CSS-local rather than encapsulated as its own exported primitive family.
Code/diff surfaces are more inspectable in Storybook now, but the component API is still relatively compact for broader docs/chat/editor compositions.

a11yRisks: The current changes do not introduce new obvious contrast regressions, but metadata hierarchy still relies heavily on CSS context rather than explicit component variants.
Diff tabs remain keyboard-friendly, though broader panel semantics are still fairly minimal for richer documentation use cases.
issues: The most important specimen surface is now reusable, but the refactor page's lower ledger rhythm still depends on page-local CSS behavior rather than a first-class primitive or configurable variant.

DiffViewer is more inspectable in Storybook, but the code surface family is still somewhat compact for broader docs/chat/editor composition because the component API does not yet expose richer panel-level metadata or empty-state framing.
Storybook coverage improved, but the new reusable surface area is still stronger on the bestiary side than on the refactor/ledger side.
nextEdits: Extract or parameterize the refactor page's lower ledger rhythm into a reusable Seraph summary/ledger variant instead of relying on page-local alignment rules.
Deepen the code/diff surface API slightly so Storybook and future docs/chat use cases can configure richer file metadata and panel framing without wrapping the component externally.
Add corresponding Storybook coverage for the new ledger/code variants so the kit improvement is inspectable outside the page preview.
strengths: The Seraph kit is materially more reusable because the bestiary artifact-first composition is no longer trapped in page-local markup.
Storybook and example-app parity remain intact because the new surface was extracted through the shared runtime path.
Dark-mode and state styling quality were preserved during the extraction instead of regressing into one-off surfaces.

instructions: Refactor and improve the kit to match specs and improve reusability across use cases. Preserve reconstructive shared surfaces, Storybook/example parity, dark mode, accessibility-sensitive states, and reusable code/editor/diff components.
Use the previous review as the authoritative list of misses.
Fix the most architecture-relevant and reuse-blocking issues first, then close visual and a11y state gaps.
Prefer shared primitives and configurable compositions over duplicating page-local structures.
prompt.role: Senior React/CSS design-system engineer in a refactor loop
prompt.task: Apply the review feedback directly and continue improving reusable kit quality without narrowing the user request.

inputs.requestText: Refactor and improve the kit to match specs and improve reusability across use cases. Preserve reconstructive shared surfaces, Storybook/example parity, dark mode, accessibility-sensitive states, and reusable code/editor/diff components.
